summary: The Docker-based reproduction is designed to demonstrate that the vulnerable configure_jndi method in JdbcConnection performs an unvalidated JNDI lookup that loads a malicious Java class from an attacker-controlled LDAP server, achieving code execution inside the JRuby container.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should start a mock LDAP server, trigger the JNDI lookup at connection.rb:29 with an attacker-controlled ldap:// URL, load the malicious Exploit class, and create /tmp/pwned.txt as proof of code execution.
actual_observed_result: PoC was not executed during this session; no runtime observation is available.
verification_method: The verify scripts check for the existence and contents of /tmp/pwned.txt inside the container. The presence of this file confirms the JNDI lookup loaded and executed the malicious class, distinguishing code execution from a generic NamingException or connection failure.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker was not available in the audit environment to execute the reproduction container.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
